pi_items.source: Task verification logic (verifier.py) and setup script (setup_task.sh) specifying file paths.
Task verification logic (verifier.py) and setup script (setup_task.sh) specifying file paths.
Direct search on the live CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) parsing HTML elements class='nfpa-health', 'nfpa-flam', and 'nfpa-react' for each specific chemical CAS page.
Confirmed by comparing the true CAMEO health ratings where Allyl alcohol has the highest health hazard rating (4).

privileged_info_summary: The expected CSV and TXT file paths were verified to be /home/ga/Desktop/isomer_hazards.csv and /home/ga/Desktop/worst_case_analysis.txt respectively. Using live data from the CAMEO Chemicals website, the true NFPA 704 ratings were verified for the 4 target chemicals: Acetone (Health: 1, Flammability: 3, Instability: 0), Allyl alcohol (Health: 4, Flammability: 3, Instability: 1), Propylene oxide (Health: 3, Flammability: 4, Instability: 2), and Propionaldehyde (Health: 2, Flammability: 3, Instability: 2). The metadata's claim that Propionaldehyde has an instability of 1 is incorrect; the actual value is 2. Based on these verified ratings, Allyl alcohol definitively has the highest health hazard rating (4), confirming it as the correct worst-case target.
